checks.finding: Every AC-NNN referenced in tasks.md (AC-001..AC-015 across T-001..T-010 Done When items, e.g. T-001 AC-004/AC-006, T-007 AC-010/AC-011/AC-015, T-008 AC-013) resolves to a row in acceptance-tests.md, which defines AC-001 through AC-015.

Every Blockers value is 'None' or a comma-separated list of valid T-NNN ids (T-002:T-001; T-007:T-005, T-006; T-009:T-007, T-008; T-010:T-001..T-009); no ranges or prose. Consistent with precheck-result.json blockers_format_valid=true.
All 12 requirements REQ-001..REQ-012 are covered by at least one task's Requirements field (e.g. REQ-005 by T-003, REQ-010 by T-009, REQ-011 by T-010, REQ-007 by T-006/T-008).
All 15 acceptance tests AC-001..AC-015 are traceable to a task via traceability.md AC->TEST->Task (e.g. AC-004->T-001, AC-009->T-006, AC-013->T-008), consistent with tasks.md Done When references.
Every task T-001..T-010 lists at least one valid REQ-NNN in its Requirements field (e.g. T-001 REQ-001/003/012, T-005 REQ-008/009, T-010 REQ-011).
No acceptance test is unmapped: the traceability AC->TEST->Task table maps every AC-001..AC-015 (via TEST-001..TEST-015) to a concrete task; no orphan rows.

Observed all 10 tasks with Approval='Approved (sudo 2026-07-05T10:33:11Z)' (parenthesized audit mark) and Status='Implementation Complete' (in the valid set); Implementation Complete requires an approved approval, which is satisfied. Lifecycle-consistent under the post-implementation lifecycle-validity rule (AGENTS.md, WFI-004).

Re-evaluated against the corrected authoritative contract (vocabulary {test-after, acceptance-first, tdd}; risk-gate-matrix low->test-after, medium->acceptance-first, high/critical->tdd): all 10 tasks carry Risk + Risk Rationale + Required Workflow, and every pairing matches the matrix exactly - high-risk T-001/T-002/T-003/T-006/T-007/T-008/T-009 -> tdd, medium-risk T-004 -> acceptance-first (tasks.md line 123, the required pairing for medium), low-risk T-005/T-010 -> test-after. The FAIL in my prior emission was an artifact of the incomplete {tdd|test-after} enum in the orchestrator's paraphrase, not a defect in tasks.md; it is withdrawn.
acceptance-tests.md lists each AC-001..AC-015 exactly once (verified by count); traceability.md AC->TEST->Task lists each AC exactly once with no conflicting duplicate mappings.

Every Blockers reference points to an existing task (all in T-001..T-010). dependency-graph.json's 10 nodes and 18 edges faithfully represent tasks.md Blockers (e.g. T-007 blocked by T-005/T-006, T-009 by T-007/T-008, T-010 by T-001..T-009).
The dependency graph is acyclic; all edges respect the strict order T-001 < T-002 < T-003 < T-004 < {T-005,T-006} < T-007 < T-008 < T-009 < T-010, yielding a valid topological ordering.

Each task has a single coherent deliverable (T-001 server foundation/probe-engine/envelope, T-004 dist bundle+CI+smoke distribution, T-007 installer Cursor/VS Code register, T-009 uninstall). No task bundles unrelated concerns.

Every Done When item is observable/verifiable (named test files, AC verifications, implementation reports), including the human-amended addenda references in T-005 (reports/implementation/local-env-mcp/T-005.md, USERGUIDE.md) and T-010 item 2 (reports/implementation/local-env-mcp/T-010.md), each naming a concrete artifact and verifiable condition.

Judged on task ids, REQ links, and AC mappings (not the frozen-artifact-vs-amended Done When prose): traceability.md Task->REQ matches every tasks.md Requirements field (e.g. T-005 REQ-008/009, T-008 REQ-007/008/009), and AC->TEST->Task task ids are consistent with tasks.md task ids.
